T1102.001.object: an existing, legitimate external Web service to host information that points to additional command and control (C2) infrastructure

T1102.001.subject: Adversaries
T1102.001.object: content, known as a dead drop resolver, on Web services with embedded (and often obfuscated/encoded) domains or IP addresses

T1102.002.subject: Adversaries
T1102.002.object: an existing, legitimate external Web service as a means for sending commands to and receiving output from a compromised system over the Web service channel

T1102.003.subject: Adversaries
T1102.003.object: an existing, legitimate external Web service as a means for sending commands to a compromised system without receiving return output over the Web service channel

T1104.subject: Adversaries
T1104.object: multiple stages for command and control that are employed under different conditions or for certain functions

T1104.subject: A second remote access tool (RAT)
T1104.object: the host to the second-stage command and control server

T1105.subject: Adversaries
T1105.object: tools or other files from an external system into a compromised environment

T1105.subject: adversaries
T1105.object: tools between victim devices within a compromised environment

T1105.subject: adversaries
T1105.object: various utilities to download tools, such as copy, finger, certutil, and PowerShell commands

T1106.subject: Adversaries
T1106.object: the native OS application programming interface (API) to execute behaviors

T1106.subject: Adversaries
T1106.object: assembly to directly or in-directly invoke syscalls in an attempt to subvert defensive sensors and detection signatures

T1106.subject: Adversaries
T1106.verb: may also attempt to tamper with
T1106.object: sensors and defensive tools associated with API monitoring, such as unhooking monitored functions

T1110.subject: Adversaries
T1110.object: brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained

T1110.subject: an adversary
T1110.object: the password using a repetitive or iterative mechanism

T1110.001.subject: Adversaries
T1110.001.object: passwords to attempt access to accounts

T1110.001.subject: an adversary
T1110.001.object: login credentials without prior knowledge of system or environment passwords during an operation by using a list of common passwords

T1110.002.subject: Adversaries
T1110.002.object: password cracking to attempt to recover usable credentials, such as plaintext passwords, when credential material such as password hashes are obtained

T1110.002.subject: the adversary
T1110.002.object: a pre-computed rainbow table to crack hashes

T1110.003.subject: Adversaries
T1110.003.object: a single or small list of commonly used passwords against many different accounts to attempt to acquire valid account credentials

T1110.004.subject: Adversaries
T1110.004.object: credentials obtained from breach dumps of unrelated accounts to gain access to target accounts through credential overlap
